name: AJCC v6 Stage
definitions: A cancer stage defined according to the AJCC 6th edition criteria.

name: AJCC v7 Stage
definitions: A cancer stage defined according to the AJCC 7th edition criteria.

name: AJCC v8 Stage
definitions: A cancer stage defined according to the AJCC 8th edition criteria.

name: APC Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in signal transduction. It is involved in both adhesion and migration of cells.

name: APC wt Allele
definitions: Human APC wild-type allele is located within 5q21-q22 and is approximately 108 kb in length. This allele, which encodes adenomatosis polyposis coli protein, is involved in the progression of Wnt protein mediated signaling. Mutations in this gene are associated with familial adenomatous polyposis, an autosomal dominant pre-malignant disease that usually progresses to malignancy.

name: AXIN2 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in signal transduction and plays a role in development.

name: AXIN2 wt Allele
definitions: Human AXIN2 wild-type allele is located within 17q23-q24 and is approximately 33 kb in length. This allele, which encodes axin-2 protein, plays a role in the inhibition of the Wnt protein signaling pathway.

name: AXIN2-Associated Polyposis
definitions: A rare autosomal dominant syndrome caused by constitutional (germline) loss-of-function variants in AXIN2 gene. It is characterized by the presence of multiple colorectal adenomatous polyps and an increased risk of colorectal carcinoma. Oligodontia and ectodermal dysplasia may or may not be present.

name: Abdominal Pain
definitions: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the abdominal region. | Discomfort in the central region of the body located between the chest and the groin. | Painful sensation in the abdominal region. | The sensation of discomfort in the abdominal region.

name: Absence of Invasion
definitions: A finding indicating the absence of invasion in a sample.

name: Adenine DNA Glycosylase
definitions: Adenine DNA glycosylase protein (546 aa, ~60 kDa) is encoded by the human MUTYH gene. This nuclear enzyme plays a role in mismatch repair via the removal of mutagenic adenine/8-oxo-guanine.

name: Adenocarcinoma in Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from an adenomatous polyp.

name: Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: A polypoid neoplasm that arises from the glandular epithelium of the gastrointestinal tract. There is a proliferation of glandular cells that may display dysplastic cytologic features. Representative examples include the adenomatous polyps of the colon and rectum.

name: Advanced Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.

name: Advanced Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: Digestive system carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.

name: Advanced Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A squamous cell carcinoma that has spread extensively to other anatomic sites or is no longer responding to treatment.

name: Aggressive Clinical Course
definitions: A finding indicating that a disease has an aggressive clinical outcome.

name: Anal Canal Paget Disease
definitions: Paget disease involving the squamous epithelium of the anal canal.

name: Anal Margin Paget Disease
definitions: Paget disease involving the perianal skin.

name: Anal Paget Disease
definitions: A slowly spreading, erythematous eczematoid plaque in the anal region.  Histologically, the basal part or whole thickness of the squamous epithelium is infiltrated by large cells with abundant pale cytoplasm and large nuclei.  Half of the cases are associated with an internal malignancy, most often a colorectal adenocarcinoma.  The other half of the cases, have a high local recurrence rate and they may become invasive (WHO).

name: Anaplastic Lesion
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate characterized by the presence of large pleomorphic cells, necrosis, and high mitotic activity in a tissue sample.

name: Anorectal Area
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: A subdivision of the digestive tract in humans that includes the rectum, the anal canal and the anus. | The distal portion of the gastrointestinal tract that includes the anal canal and rectum.

name: Antigen Peptide Transporter 1
definitions: Antigen peptide transporter 1 (808 aa, ~87 kDa) is encoded by the human TAP1 gene. This protein plays a role in cytoplasmic antigen transport and subsequent presentation by MHC class I molecules.

name: Attenuated Familial Adenomatous Polyposis
definitions: A rare genetic syndrome with an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance. It was formerly called hereditary flat adenoma syndrome (HFAS) but is now recognized as an attenuated variant of familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP). It is most commonly associated with mutations in the APC gene specifically: exons 1-5, exon 9 and in the distal 3' terminus. Clinical presentation occurs in the middle decades and is characterized by the proximal development of multiple (but less than 100), non-polypoid colonic adenomas. Clinical progression to colorectal cancer may occur later in life but the risk is less than with classic FAP.

name: BAX Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of apoptosis by binding p53 protein, which accelerates this mechanism of cell death.

name: BAX wt Allele
definitions: Human BAX wild-type allele is located within 19q13.33 and is approximately 7 kb in length. This allele, which encodes apoptosis regulator BAX protein, plays a role in the regulation of the apoptotic program by varying the permeability of the mitochondrial membrane.

name: BCL10 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the promotion of apoptosis.

name: BCL10 wt Allele
definitions: Human BCL10 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 1p22 and is approximately 12 kb in length. This allele, which encodes B-cell lymphoma/leukemia 10 protein, is involved in the induction of apoptosis and the activation of NF-kappaB.

name: BIRC5 Gene
definitions: This gene regulates the apoptotic pathway by manipulation of the cell cycle. BIRC5 has been associated with several cancers.

name: BIRC5 wt Allele
definitions: Human BIRC5 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 17q25 and is approximately 10 kb in length. This allele, which encodes baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein 5, is involved in the prevention of apoptotic cell death.

name: BRAF Gene
definitions: A gene that makes a protein called B-RAF, which is involved in sending signals in cells and in cell growth. This gene may be mutated (changed) in many types of cancer, which causes a change in the B-RAF protein. This can increase the growth and spread of cancer cells. | This gene is involved in cell organization/biogenesis and the inhibition of apoptosis.

name: BRAF Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of the BRAF gene.

name: BRAF wt Allele
definitions: Human BRAF wild-type allele is located within 7q34 and is approximately 190 kb in length. This allele, which encodes B-Raf proto-oncogene serine/threonine-protein kinase protein, plays a role in the transduction of mitogenic signals from the cell membrane to the nucleus. A specific activating somatic mutation in the BRAF gene is associated with several types of cancer such as colorectal and thyroid carcinomas and malignant melanoma.

name: BRCA1-Related Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
definitions: An autosomal dominant inherited syndrome caused by deleterious mutations in the BRCA1 gene. Affected individuals are at high risk of developing breast cancer, ovarian cancer, and other cancers including cervical, endometrial, fallopian tube, and colon cancer.

name: BRINP1 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the inhibition of cell proliferation.

name: BRINP1 wt Allele
definitions: Human BRINP1 wild-type allele is located within 9q32-q33 and is approximately 203 kb in length. This allele, which encodes BMP/retinoic acid-inducible neural-specific protein 1, is involved in the modulation of cellular proliferation, the inhibition of the G1/S transition of the cell cycle, the mediation of non-apoptotic cell death and the regulation of the expression of components of the fibrinolysis pathway. Hypermethylation-based silencing or loss of homozygosity of the gene occurs in many cases of bladder cancer.

name: BUB1 Gene
definitions: This gene is a modulator of the mitotic spindle cell cycle checkpoint.

name: BUB1 wt Allele
definitions: Human BUB1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 2q14 and is approximately 40 kb in length. This allele, which encodes mitotic checkpoint serine/threonine-protein kinase BUB1 protein, is involved in the regulation of spindle checkpoints and mitotic integrity. Mutations in this gene, which generate allelic variants have been associated with aneuploidy and several forms of cancer.

name: Baculoviral IAP Repeat-Containing Protein 5
definitions: Baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein 5 (142 aa, ~16 kDa) is encoded by the human BIRC5 gene. This protein plays a role in the regulation of both apoptosis and the cell cycle.

name: Benign Cellular Infiltrate
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a cellular infiltrate without atypia in a tumor sample.

name: Bevacizumab/Capecitabine Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of bevacizumab and capecitabine that may be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, small bowel , ampullary and appendiceal adenocarcinoma.

name: Bevacizumab/Fluorouracil Bolus/Leucovorin Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of bolus fluorouracil, leucovorin and bevacizumab that is used for the treatment of colorectal cancer and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: Bevacizumab/Irinotecan Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan and bevacizumab that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, appendiceal adenocarcinoma, and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: Bloom Syndrome
definitions: A rare, inherited disorder marked by height that is shorter than average, a narrow face with redness and a rash, a high-pitched voice, and fertility problems. Patients with this disorder have an increased risk of cancer, especially leukemia and osteosarcoma (bone cancer). Bloom syndrome is caused by changes in a protein that normally helps cells make copies of the DNA. Changes in this protein cause many breaks, rearrangements, and other mutations in the DNA. It is a type of autosomal recessive genetic disease. | Bloom syndrome is an autosomal recessive disorder associated with mutations in BLM gene encoding protein that belongs to the family of DNA helicases. It is characterized by predisposition to cancer, immunodeficiency, high sensitivity to UV and genomic instability of somatic cells. (Oncogene 2001 Dec 13;20(57):8276-80)

name: Bolus Fluorouracil/Leucovorin Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of bolus fluorouracil and leucovorin that is used for the treatment of colon cancer, and ampullary, appendiceal, small bowel, and pancreatic adenocarcinoma.

name: Breast Cancer Type 1 Susceptibility Protein
definitions: Breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein (1863 aa, ~208 kDa) is encoded by the human BRCA1 gene. This protein plays a role in both the maintenance of DNA and the mediation of ubiquitin E3 ligase activity.

name: CAPOX Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and oxaliplatin that can be used for the treatment of ampullary, appendiceal, pancreatic and small bowel adenocarcinomas, anal, colorectal, gastric, esophageal and esophagogastric junction cancers, certain neuroendocrine tumors, occult primary tumors and mucinous carcinoma of the ovary. Low-dose CapeOX may be used for the treatment of gastric, esophageal and esophagogastric junction cancers.

name: CAPOX-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of bevacizumab, capecitabine and oxaliplatin that may be used in the treatment of colorectal cancer, small bowel, appendiceal, and ampullary adenocarcinoma and mucinous carcinoma of the ovary.

name: CAPOX-Cetuximab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of cetuximab, capecitabine and oxaliplatin that may be used in the treatment of colorectal cancer and appendiceal cancer.

name: CAPOX-Cetuximab/Encorafenib Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and oxaliplatin (CAPOX), cetuximab and encorafenib that may be used in the treatment of colon cancer.

name: CAPOX-Encorafenib/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and oxaliplatin (CAPOX), encorafenib and panitumumab that may be used in the treatment of colon cancer.

name: CAPOX/Atezolizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of atezolizumab, capecitabine, and oxaliplatin that can be used for the treatment of colon cancer, and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: CDH1 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in cell-cell adhesion and loss of function contributes to the progression of many carcinomas.

name: CDH1 wt Allele
definitions: Human CDH1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 16q22.1 and is approximately 98 kb in length. This allele, which encodes cadherin-1 protein, plays a role in cell-cell adhesion and cell-matrix adhesion.

name: CEACAM7 Gene
definitions: This gene is postulated to play a role in cell adhesion, however this role has not been substantiated. The gene is also a member of the immunoglobulin superfamily.

name: CEACAM7 wt Allele
definitions: Human CEACAM7 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 19q13.2 and is approximately 15 kb in length. This allele, which encodes carcinoembryonic antigen-related cell adhesion molecule 7 protein, is involved in tumor suppression and cellular growth.

name: CHEK2-Related Hereditary Cancer Predisposition Syndrome
definitions: An autosomal dominant cancer predisposition syndrome caused by germline mutations of the CHEK2 gene. It is associated with breast carcinoma, gastric carcinoma, colorectal carcinoma, thyroid gland carcinoma, kidney carcinoma, prostate carcinoma, and non-Hodgkin lymphoma.

name: CHKA Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the biosynthesis of phosphatidylcholine.

name: CHKA wt Allele
definitions: Human CHKA wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 11q13.2 and is approximately 69 kb in length. This allele, which encodes choline kinase alpha protein, plays a role in the initiation and regulation of phosphatidylcholine biosynthesis. The CHKA gene is overexpressed in several types of cancer such as breast, colon, lung and prostate cancers.

name: CPTAC Codelists Terminology
definitions: Terminology used to support the various lists of data useful for tracking trends by CPTAC.

name: CPTAC Medical Conditions Codelist
definitions: Terminology used in support of the data collection efforts of the Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium (CPTAC) with the focus on medical conditions.

name: CPTAC Neoplasms Codelist
definitions: Terminology used in support of the data collection efforts of the Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium (CPTAC) with the focus on neoplasms.

name: CPTAC Terminology
definitions: Terminology used in support of the data collection efforts of the Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium (CPTAC).

name: CSMD1 Gene
definitions: This gene may play a role in complement activation.

name: CSMD1 wt Allele
definitions: Human CSMD1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 8p23.2 and is approximately 2,059 kb in length. This allele, which encodes CUB and sushi domain-containing protein 1, may be involved in the modulation of complement. Mutation or deletion of the gene is associated with invasive ductal breast carcinoma, colorectal adenocarcinoma and head and neck carcinomas.

name: CTRP Disease Terminology
definitions: A set of terminology that includes the diseases and neoplastic processes used in NCI-sponsored clinical trials.

name: CTRP Terminology
definitions: Terminology that is used to support NCI's Clinical Trials Reporting Program (CTRP), a database that captures the required reporting data for all NCI-sponsored clinical trials.

name: CTS-API Disease Main Type Terminology
definitions: Terminology subset used for the purpose of supporting the CTS-API disease main type terminology.

name: CUB and Sushi Domain-Containing Protein 1
definitions: CUB and sushi domain-containing protein 1 (3565 aa, ~389 kDa) is encoded by the human CSMD1 gene. This protein may play a role in the activation of complement.

name: Cadherin-1
definitions: Cadherin-1 (882 aa, ~97 kDa) is encoded by the human CHD1 gene. This protein plays a role in the regulation of cell-cell adhesion, proliferation, and motility of epithelial cells

name: CapOX/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine, oxaliplatin, and panitumumab that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer and appendiceal cancer.

name: Capecitabine/Cetuximab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and cetuximab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: Capecitabine/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and panitumumab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: Carcinoembryonic Antigen-Related Cell Adhesion Molecule 7
definitions: Carcinoembryonic antigen-related cell adhesion molecule 7 (265 aa, ~29 kDa) is encoded by the human CEACAM7 gene. This protein may play a role in tumor suppresssion.

name: Carcinomatous Component Present
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a malignant epithelial component in a tumor sample.

name: Catenin Beta-1
definitions: Catenin beta-1 (781 aa, ~85 kDa) is encoded by the human CTNNB1 gene. This protein is involved in Wnt signaling, adherens junction structure, and the regulation of cell growth.

name: Cecum
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: A blind pouch-like commencement of the colon in the right lower quadrant of the abdomen at the end of the small intestine and the start of the large intestine. | A pouch in the digestive tract that connects the ileum with the ascending colon of the large intestine. It is separated from the ileum by the ileocecal valve, and is the beginning of the large intestine. It is also separated from the colon by the cecocolic junction. | A pouch that forms the first part of the large intestine. It connects the small intestine to the colon, which is part of the large intestine. | The pouch-like portion of the proximal large intestine opening into the colon.

name: Cell
definitions: The individual unit that makes up all of the tissues of the body. All living things are made up of one or more cells. | The smallest units of living structure capable of independent existence, composed of a membrane-enclosed mass of protoplasm and containing a nucleus or nucleoid.

name: Cellosaurus Disease Terminology
definitions: The Cellosaurus Disease Terminology is a set of terms used to annotate cell lines originating from a diseased patient or animal. For inherited diseases, the relevant terms are only applied to cell lines originating from individuals suffering or at risk for a disease and not for individuals that are carriers for such disease. For cancers, the relevant terms are only applied to cell lines established from a cancerous tissue. The Cellosaurus is developed by Dr. Amos Bairoch of the CALIPHO group at the Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics.

name: Cellular Tumor Antigen p53
definitions: Cellular tumor antigen p53 (393 aa, ~44 kDa) is encoded by the human TP53 gene. This protein plays a role in the regulation of both the cell cycle and apoptosis.

name: Cetuximab/Irinotecan Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan and cetuximab that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, and appendiceal cancer.

name: Cetuximab/Irinotecan/Vemurafenib Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of cetuximab, irinotecan and vemurafenib that can be used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: Childhood Carcinoma
definitions: A rare carcinoma that occurs during childhood.

name: Childhood Lesion
definitions: A benign or malignant lesion arising during childhood.

name: Childhood Malignant Intestinal Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the intestine and occurs during childhood.

name: Childhood Malignant Neoplasm
definitions: A malignant neoplasm that occurs during childhood. Representative examples include acute leukemias, malignant brain neoplasms, and sarcomas.

name: Childhood Neoplasm
definitions: A benign or malignant neoplasm that occurs during childhood.

name: Choline Kinase Alpha
definitions: Choline kinase alpha (457 aa, ~52 kDa) is encoded by the human CHKA gene. This protein plays a role in phospholipid biosynthesis.

name: Chromogranin-A Positive Neoplastic Cells Present
definitions: An immunophenotypic test result indicating positive staining of neoplastic cells for chromogranin A.

name: Clinical Trials Search-Application Programming Interface Disease Category Terminology
definitions: Terminology subsets used for the purpose of supporting the CTS-API.

name: Cloacal Membrane
definitions: The membrane covering the embryonic cloaca; it is composed from ectoderm and endoderm, and, following cloacal folding during embryonic development, the membrane ruptures and is absorbed, becoming continuous with the rectum.

name: Colon
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: The longest part of the large intestine, which is a tube-like organ connected to the small intestine at one end and the anus at the other. The colon removes water and some nutrients and electrolytes from partially digested food. The remaining material, solid waste called stool, moves through the colon to the rectum and leaves the body through the anus. | The part of the large intestine measured from the cecum to the rectum consisting of ascending, transverse, descending and sigmoid portions. The purpose of the colon is to remove water from digested food prior to excretion. | The portion of the large intestine which extends from the cecum (or small intestine in animals that don't have a cecum) to the rectum.

name: Colon Adenoma
definitions: An adenoma that arises from the colon. The group of colon adenomas includes conventional adenomas and traditional serrated adenomas.

name: Colon Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: A polypoid adenoma that arises from, and protrudes into the lumen of, the colon.

name: Colon Neuroendocrine Neoplasm
definitions: A neoplasm with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon. It includes neuroendocrine tumors (well-differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms) and neuroendocrine carcinomas (poorly differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms).

name: Colorectal Mutant Cancer Protein
definitions: Colorectal mutant cancer protein (829 aa, ~93 kDa) is encoded by the human MCC gene. This protein may be involved in modulation of the cell cycle.

name: Colorectal Neuroendocrine Neoplasm
definitions: A neoplasm with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the colon or rectum. It includes neuroendocrine tumors (well-differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms) and neuroendocrine carcinomas (poorly differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms).

name: Colorectal Region
definitions: The region that includes the colon and the rectum.

name: Comedo Necrosis
definitions: A morphologic finding that indicates the presence of a cancerous tumor with a central area that contains necrotic cancer cells.

name: Constipation
definitions: A condition in which stool becomes hard, dry, and difficult to pass, and bowel movements don't happen very often. Other symptoms may include painful bowel movements, and feeling bloated, uncomfortable, and sluggish. | A disorder characterized by irregular and infrequent or difficult evacuation of the bowels. | Irregular and infrequent or difficult evacuation of the bowels.

name: Constitutional Mismatch Repair Deficiency Syndrome
definitions: A rare childhood cancer predisposition syndrome caused by biallelic inheritance of mutations in MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, or PMS2 genes. It is characterized by the development of childhood cancers, usually hematological malignancies and/or brain tumors, and colorectal cancers with multiple intestinal polyps. The majority of patients show signs of neurofibromatosis type 1.

name: Cribriform Comedo-Type Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant cribriform glands with central necrotic changes.

name: Cribriform Pattern
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of sheets of malignant epithelial cells punctuated by gland-like spaces in a tissue sample. | Formation of epithelial pseudoglandular structures with lumens.

name: Crohn Disease
definitions: A chronic, transmural inflammation that can affect any location along the gastrointestinal tract. | A condition in which the gastrointestinal tract is inflamed over a long period of time. Crohn disease usually affects the small intestine and colon. Symptoms include fever, diarrhea, stomach cramps, vomiting, and weight loss. Crohn disease increases the risk of colorectal cancer and small intestine cancer. It is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). | A condition in which the gastrointestinal tract is inflamed over a long period of time. Regional enteritis usually affects the small intestine and colon. Symptoms include fever, diarrhea, stomach cramps, vomiting, and weight loss. Regional enteritis increases the risk of colorectal cancer and small intestine cancer. It is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). | A gastrointestinal disorder characterized by chronic inflammation involving all layers of the intestinal wall, noncaseating granulomas affecting the intestinal wall and regional lymph nodes, and transmural fibrosis. Crohn disease most commonly involves the terminal ileum; the colon is the second most common site of involvement.

name: DCC Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the regulation of several processes that determine neuronal cell morphology.

name: DCC wt Allele
definitions: Human DCC wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 18q21.3 and is approximately 1190 kb in length. This allele, which encodes netrin receptor DCC protein, plays a role in both axonal guidance and neuronal growth cone formation. Mutation of this gene may be associated with both colorectal and esophageal carcinomas.

name: DNA Repair and Recombination Protein RAD54-Like
definitions: DNA repair and recombination protein RAD54-like (747 aa, ~84 kDa) is encoded by the human RAD54L gene. This protein plays a role in both DNA repair and mitotic recombination.

name: Digestive System
definitions: The organs that take in food and turn it into products that the body can use to stay healthy. Waste products the body cannot use leave the body through bowel movements. The digestive system includes the salivary glands, mouth, esophagus, stomach, liver, pancreas, gallbladder, small and large intestines, and rectum. | The system that includes the esophagus, stomach, small and large intestine, anus, liver, biliary tract, and pancreas. | The system that includes the esophagus, stomach, small and large intestine, anus, liver, biliary tract, and pancreas. (NCI)

name: Digestive System Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from any part of the digestive system.

name: Digestive System Adenosquamous Carcinoma
definitions: A rare carcinoma that arises from the digestive system and is characterized by the presence of adenocarcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma components.

name: Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant neoplasm that arises from the epithelium of any part of the digestive system.  Representative examples include colorectal carcinoma, esophageal carcinoma, and pancreatic carcinoma.

name: Digestive System Carcinoma In Situ
definitions: An intraepithelial carcinoma arising from any part of the digestive system without evidence of invasion.

name: Digestive System Large Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the digestive system and is composed of malignant large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20% (often more than 70%).

name: Digestive System Mixed Adenoneuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the digestive system and is characterized by the presence of a malignant glandular epithelial component and a malignant neuroendocrine component. At least 30% of either component should be present for the diagnosis to be made.

name: Digestive System Small Cell Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the digestive system and is composed of malignant small cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20% (often more than 70%).

name: Digestive System Undifferentiated Carcinoma
definitions: A usually aggressive carcinoma that arises from the digestive system and is composed of malignant cells which do not display evidence of glandular, squamous, or transitional cell differentiation.

name: Distal Colon
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: The part of colon that includes the splenic flexure, descending colon, and sigmoid colon.

name: EPCAM Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in calcium-independent cellular adhesion and in the regulation of immune responses.

name: EPCAM wt Allele
definitions: Human EPCAM wild-type allele is located within 2p21 and is approximately 18 kb in length. This allele, which encodes epithelial cell adhesion molecule protein, is involved in calcium-independent cellular adhesion.

name: Embryonic Cell
definitions: A cell formed in the initial stages of embryonic development, after zygote formation.

name: Endocrine Cell of the Digestive System
Endocrine Differentiation
definitions: A finding in a tissue sample indicating the presence of epithelial cells that synthesize and secrete hormones.

name: Endocrine Gland
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: Ductless glands that secrete substances which are released directly into the circulation and which influence metabolism and other body functions. | Endocrine glands are glands of the endocrine system that secrete their products directly into the circulatory system rather than through a duct.

name: Endocrine System
definitions: A system of glands and cells that make hormones that are released directly into the blood and travel to tissues and organs all over the body. The endocrine system controls growth, sexual development, sleep, hunger, and the way the body uses food. | Anatomical system that consists of the glands and parts of glands that produce endocrine secretions and help to integrate and control bodily metabolic activity. | The body system that comprises the organs and glands that produce hormones that are secreted into the blood and control physiological functions that include reproduction, growth and development, metabolism, and stress responses.

name: Endothelial Cell
definitions: The main type of cell forming the lining of blood and lymph vessels and the inner layer of the endocardium. | The main type of cell found in the inside lining of blood vessels, lymph vessels, and the heart.

name: Epithelial Cell
definitions: Any of the cells of the epithelium, which covers the body and lines its cavities and glands.

name: Epithelial Cell Adhesion Molecule
semantic_types: Amino Acid, Peptide, or Protein|Immunologic Factor
definitions: Epithelial cell adhesion molecule (314 aa, ~35 kDa) is encoded by the human EPCAM gene. This protein is involved in both cell adhesion and cellular proliferation.

name: Epithelial Component Present
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of an epithelial component.

name: Expression of Neuroendocrine Markers Present
definitions: A finding indicating expression of neuroendocrine markers in a tumor sample.

name: Extensive Stage Lesion
definitions: A lesion that has spread outside of the immediate area where it originated.

name: Extracellular Mucin Present
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of extracellular mucin in a tumor sample.

name: Extrahepatic Bile Duct
definitions: Passages external to the liver for the conveyance of bile. These include the common bile duct and the common hepatic duct. | The part of the common hepatic bile duct (tube that collects bile from the liver) that is outside the liver. This duct joins a duct from the gallbladder to form the common bile duct, which carries bile into the small intestine when food is being digested. | The portion of the biliary tract outside the liver; the common hepatic duct joins the cystic duct to form the common bile duct. | The portion of the biliary tract outside the liver; the common hepatic duct joins the cystic duct to form the common bile duct. (NCI)

name: FGFR4 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in mitogenesis and differentiation.

name: FGFR4 wt Allele
definitions: Human FGFR4 wild-type allele is located within 5q35.1-qter and is approximately 11 kb in length. This allele, which encodes fibroblast growth factor receptor 4 protein, plays a role in the regulation of mitogenesis and differentiation.

name: FOLFIRI Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin calcium (calcium folinate), 5-fluorouracil, and irinotecan that can be used in the treatment of ampullary, appendiceal, small bowel, pancreatic and occult primary adenocarcinoma; colorectal, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric, and hepatobiliary cancers; well-differentiated grade 3 neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), and extrapulmonary poorly differentiated neuroendocrine carcinoma, large or small cell carcinoma and mixed neuroendocrine-non-neuroendocrine neoplasm. | An abbreviation for a chemotherapy combination used to treat advanced colorectal cancer that has spread. It is also being studied in the treatment of other types of cancer. It includes the drugs leucovorin calcium (folinic acid), fluorouracil, and irinotecan hydrochloride.

name: FOLFIRI-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A chemotherapy combination used as an initial treatment of colorectal cancer that has spread. It includes the drugs leucovorin (folinic acid), fluorouracil, irinotecan hydrochloride, and bevacizumab. | A regimen consisting of bevacizumab, fluorouracil, irinotecan and leucovorin that may be used in the treatment of ampullary and appendiceal adenocarcinoma, colorectal cancer, and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: FOLFIRI-Cetuximab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin (folinic acid), 5-fluorouracil, and irinotecan plus cetuximab that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer.

name: FOLFIRI-Cetuximab/Encorafenib Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin (folinic acid), 5-fluorouracil, and irinotecan (FOLFIRI) in combination with cetuximab and encorafenib that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer.

name: FOLFIRI-Encorafenib/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin (folinic acid), 5-fluorouracil, and irinotecan (FOLFIRI) in combination with encorafenib and panitumumab that can be used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: FOLFIRI-Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and irinotecan (FOLFIRI), plus panitumumab that is used for the treatment of colorectal cancer.

name: FOLFIRI-Ramucirumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and irinotecan (FOLFIRI), plus ramucirumab that is used for the treatment of appendiceal adenocarcinoma, colon, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric and rectal cancers.

name: FOLFIRI-Ziv-aflibercept Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and irinotecan (FOLFIRI), plus ziv-aflibercept that is used for the treatment of appendiceal adenocarcinoma and colorectal cancer.

name: FOLFOX-4 Regimen
definitions: A regimen comsisting of 5-fluorouracil, leucovorin and oxaliplatin, given on a 14-day cycle, that can be used for the treatment of ampullary and pancreatic adenocarcinoma, and biliary tract, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric, and colorectal cancers.

name: FOLFOX-6 Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, infusional fluorouracil and oxaliplatin used for the treatment of advanced-stage and metastatic colorectal cancer. This particular version differs from the original FOLFOX (FOLFOX4) regimen with regards to both the doses and the administration of the three-drug combination. An alternative version, referred to as modified-FOLFOX6, is also used in clinical practice.

name: FOLFOX-7 Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, infusional fluorouracil and oxaliplatin used for the treatment of advanced-stage and metastatic colorectal cancer. This version differs from the original FOLFOX (FOLFOX4) regimen with regards to both the doses and the administration of the three-drug combination.

name: FOLFOX-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (FOLFOX), plus bevacizumab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer and mucinous neoplasms of the ovary.

name: FOLFOX-Cetuximab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (FOLFOX), plus cetuximab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: FOLFOX-Cetuximab/Encorafenib Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (FOLFOX) plus cetuximab and encorafenib that is used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, appendiceal cancer, and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: FOLFOX-Encorafenib/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (FOLFOX) plus encorafenib and panitumumab that is used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, appendiceal cancer, and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: FOLFOX-Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, fluorouracil and oxaliplatin (FOLFOX), plus panitumumab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: FOLFOXIRI Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin and irinotecan (FOLFOXIRI) that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: FOLFOXIRI-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin and irinotecan (FOLFOXIRI), plus bevacizumab that is used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: FOLFOXIRI-Cetuximab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin and irinotecan (FOLFOXIRI), plus cetuximab that may be used in the treatment of certain types of colon and rectal cancers, and appendiceal cancer.

name: FOLFOXIRI-Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin and irinotecan (FOLFOXIRI), plus panitumumab that may be used in the treatment of certain types of colon and rectal cancers, and appendiceal cancer.

name: FU-LV Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil and leucovorin, used in both the adjuvant and metastatic disease setting, for the treatment of colorectal cancer, cervical cancer, biliary tract cancer, and vaginal cancer. This regimen is also combined with radiation for the treatment of colorectal, gastric and esophageal cancer. | An abbreviation for a chemotherapy combination used with other types of therapy to treat colorectal cancer. It includes the drugs fluorouracil and leucovorin. The Mayo Clinic regimen, Roswell Park regimen, and de Gramont regimen are different in the doses and times at which the two drugs are given.

name: FU-LV-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of fluorouracil, leucovorin, and bevacizumab used for the treatment of advanced-stage colorectal cancer.

name: FUOX Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of 5-fluorouracil and oxaliplatin that may be used for the treatment of colorectal, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric and hepatobiliary cancers.

name: Familial Adenomatous Polyposis
definitions: An autosomal dominant disorder, characterized by the presence of multiple adenomas in the colon and rectum. It is caused by a germline mutation in the adenomatous polyposis coli (APC) gene which is located on the long arm of chromosome 5. The adenomas are most often tubular, and they have the tendency to progress to adenocarcinoma. They can occur throughout the colon, but they tend to concentrate in the rectum and sigmoid colon. The colorectal adenomas are detected during endoscopic examination between the age of 10 and 20 years. The adenomas increase in size and numbers with age, and there is usually progression of one or more adenomas to adenocarcinoma. The mean age of development of adenocarcinoma is about 40 years. Signs include rectal bleeding and mucousy diarrhea. | An inherited condition in which numerous polyps (growths that protrude from mucous membranes) form on the inside walls of the colon and rectum. It increases the risk for colon cancer.

name: Family Cancer History Relative Primary Diagnosis Question
definitions: A request to report the primary diagnosis for family members who were diagnosed with a neoplastic disease.

name: Favorable Clinical Outcome
definitions: A beneficial result in response to clinical management.

name: Fever
definitions: A disorder characterized by elevation of the body's temperature above the upper limit of normal. | An increase in body temperature above normal (98.6 degrees F), usually caused by disease. | Elevation of body temperature above normal due to inflammatory or immune responses. | Elevation of body temperature above normal due to the bodies own responses. | Elevation of body temperature above normal due to the production of more heat than the body is able to dissipate. | Temperature greater than or equal to 100.4 degrees F (38.0 degrees C) | The elevation of the body's temperature above the upper limit of normal, usually taken as 37.7 degrees Celsius.

name: Fluorouracil Infusional/Leucovorin Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of infusional fluorouracil and leucovorin that is used for the treatment of colon, vaginal, and gastric cancers, ampullary adenocarcinoma, and thymomas and thymic carcinomas.

name: GDC Terminology
definitions: Terminology that supports the Genomic Data Commons (GDC).

name: GDC Value Terminology
definitions: Terminology that supports the values for properties in the Genomic Data Commons (GDC) data model.

name: Gardner Syndrome
definitions: A variant of familial adenomatous polyposis. It is an autosomal dominant syndrome characterized by multiple colonic polyps predisposing to carcinoma of the colon, osteomas of the skull, epidermoid cysts, and fibromas. It is associated with mutation of the APC gene.

name: Glandular Cell
definitions: An epithelial cell which may release secretions to a free epithelial surface (exocrine) or to the circulatory system (endocrine).

name: Glandular Differentiation
definitions: A microscopic finding indicating the presence of cells with glandular differentiation in a tumor sample.

name: Glial Cell
definitions: A non-neuronal cell of the nervous system. Glial cells have diverse roles, including in regulation of neuronal survival and differentiation, maintenance of ion and neurotransmitter concentrations in the neuronal environment, synapse formation, and support of synaptic interactions and electrical signal propagation. | Any of the cells that hold nerve cells in place and help them work the way they should. The types of neuroglia include oligodendrocytes, astrocytes, microglia, and ependymal cells.

name: Goblet Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A rare adenocarcinoma of the digestive system that exhibits neuroendocrine differentiation and is associated with the presence of neoplastic signet-ring cells resembling goblet cells of the intestine.

name: Grade 1
definitions: A morphologic qualifier indicating that a cancerous lesion is well differentiated. | A term used to describe cells that look nearly normal under a microscope. These cells are less likely to grow and spread more quickly than cells in high-grade cancer or in growths that may become cancer.

name: Grade 2
definitions: A morphologic qualifier indicating that a cancerous lesion is moderately differentiated.

name: Grade 3
definitions: A morphologic qualifier indicating that a cancerous lesion is poorly differentiated.

name: Hepatobiliary System
definitions: Having to do with the liver, bile ducts, and/or gallbladder. | The body system that includes the liver, gallbladder, and associated ducts. | The liver, gallbladder, and associated ducts, considered collectively.

name: Hepatoid Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma with morphologic characteristics similar to hepatocellular carcinoma, arising from an anatomic site other than the liver.

name: Hereditary Colorectal Cancer Syndrome
definitions: A group of hereditary neoplastic syndromes that affect the colon and rectum. Included in this grouping are hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer (HNPCCC) , familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), and the hamartomatous polyposis syndromes.

name: Hereditary Colorectal Cancer Type X
definitions: Hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer syndrome characterized by the absence of germline mutations in DNA mismatch-repair genes.

name: Hereditary Lesion
definitions: A pathologic process inherited in a familial pattern.

name: Hereditary Malignant Neoplasm
definitions: Cancer that occurs in families more often than would be expected by chance.  These cancers often occur at an early age, and may indicate the presence of a gene mutation that increases the risk of cancer. They may also be a sign of shared environmental or lifestyle factors. | Malignant neoplasms occurring in families at a rate greater than that expected by chance and caused by germline mutations in a specific gene.

name: Hereditary Mixed Polyposis Syndrome
definitions: A rare autosomal dominant colorectal neoplasm predisposition syndrome caused by duplications in the regulatory region upstream of the GREM1 gene. It is characterized by the development of colorectal polyps of different histologies, individual colorectal polyps that contain components of more than one histological type, and a predisposition to colorectal adenocarcinoma.

name: Hereditary Nonpolyposis Colorectal Cancer Syndrome
definitions: An inherited disorder in which affected individuals have a higher-than-normal chance of developing colorectal cancer and certain other types of cancer, often before the age of 50. | An inherited syndrome characterized by the development of several cancers, particularly colon and rectal cancers.  It includes Lynch syndrome which is associated with germline mutations in DNA mismatch-repair genes and familial colorectal cancer type X which is characterized by the absence of germline mutations in DNA mismatch-repair genes.

name: High Grade
definitions: A term used to describe cells that look abnormal under a microscope. These cells are more likely to grow and spread quickly than cells in low-grade cancer or in growths that may become cancer. | Used to describe tumor samples that exhibit poorly differentiated or undifferentiated cells. They are generally expected to be fast growing and aggressive.

name: High Mitotic Activity
definitions: The presence of a high number of mitotic figures in a malignant tumor sample. It is associated with a higher rate of tumor recurrence and distant metastases, and a shorter survival.

name: High-Frequency Microsatellite Instability
definitions: The presence of genomic instability associated with defective DNA mismatch repair in greater than 30% of the microsatellite instability (MSI) biomarkers in a tumor sample.

name: Histology ICD-O Morphology Code
definitions: Codes for the histologic type of the tumor being reported using ICD-O.

name: Hypermutated Sample
definitions: A molecular finding indicating that the mutation rate is higher than 10 mutations per megabase (Mb) in a tissue sample.

name: ICD-O-3 Morphology Term Diagnosis Question
definitions: A request to report the ICD-O-3 morphology term that represents the neoplastic disease diagnosis for an individual.

name: IFL Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan, fluorouracil and leucovorin, all administered as bolus doses, and used for the treatment of advanced stage colorectal cancer.

name: IFL-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan, fluorouracil, leucovorin and bevacizumab used for the treatment of advanced stage colorectal cancer.

name: IFL-Douillard Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan, infusional fluorouracil and leucovorin used for the treatment of advanced stage colorectal cancer. This regimen differs from the FOLFIRI regimen with regards to the dose of fluorouracil administered.

name: IFL-FOLFIRI Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan, infusional fluorouracil and leucovorin used for the treatment of advanced stage colorectal cancer. This regimen differs from the Douillard regimen with regards to the dose of fluorouracil administered.

name: ING2 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the modulation of both protein acetylation and apoptosis.

name: ING2 wt Allele
definitions: Human ING2 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 4q35.1 and is approximately 6 kb in length. This allele, which encodes inhibitor of growth protein 2, is involved in the regulation of apoptosis and protein acetylation. Aberrant expression of the gene is associated with colon tumors.

name: IROX-Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of bevacizumab, irinotecan and oxaliplatin that can be used for the treatment of appendiceal adenocarcinoma and colorectal cancer.

name: In Situ Lesion
definitions: A morphologic finding that usually refers either to intraepithelial dysplastic and architectural changes that involve the entire thickness of the epithelium without invasion of the basement membrane (carcinoma in situ) or proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the dermal-epidermal junction without infiltration of the papillary or reticular dermis (lentigo maligna).

name: Inactivating APC Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of the APC gene that either inhibits expression or results in the translation of an inactive adenomatosis polyposis coli protein.

name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
definitions: A general term that refers to the inflammation of the colon and rectum. Inflammatory bowel disease includes ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease. | A spectrum of small and large bowel inflammatory diseases of unknown etiology. It includes Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, and colitis of indeterminate type. | Chronic inflammatory process affecting the gastrointestinal tract; primarily ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease.

name: Infusional Fluorouracil/Leucovorin/Bevacizumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of infusional fluorouracil, leucovorin and bevacizumab that is used for the treatment of colorectal cancer, and ampullary, appendiceal and small bowel adenocarcinoma.

name: Inhibitor of Growth Protein 2
definitions: Inhibitor of growth protein 2 (280 aa, ~33 kDa) is encoded by the human ING2 gene. This protein plays a role in apoptosis through the modulation of the acetylation of tumor protein 53.

name: Intermediate Grade Neuroendocrine Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic lesion with neuroendocrine differentiation and histological features of intermediate grade.

name: Intestinal Low Grade Tubuloglandular Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An uncommon, low-grade adenocarcinoma that arises from the intestine and is characterized by the presence of extremely well differentiated malignant small or medium sized glands with round or tubular shape and absence of desmoplastic stroma formation. It typically occurs in patients with long-standing inflammatory bowel disease and approximately 10% of inflammatory bowel disease-associated intestinal carcinomas are low-grade tubuloglandular adenocarcinomas.

name: Intestinal Metaplasia
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the replacement of epithelial tissue outside the intestines by intestinal-type epithelium.

name: Intestinal Neuroendocrine Carcinoma
definitions: An aggressive high-grade carcinoma with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the small or large intestine. It is composed of malignant small or large cells. The mitotic count is more than 20 per 2 mm2 and/or the Ki-67 index is more than 20%.

name: Intestine
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: Segment of the alimentary canal extending from the stomach to the anus and, in humans and other mammals, consists of two segments, the small intestine and the large intestine. | The long, tube-shaped organ in the abdomen that completes the process of digestion. The intestine has two parts, the small intestine and the large intestine. | The portion of the gastrointestinal tract between the stomach and the anus. It includes the small intestine and large intestine. | The portion of the gastrointestinal tract that includes the small and large intestines.

name: Intramucosal Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A neoplastic lesion that shows morphologic evidence of invasion into the lamina propria or muscularis mucosa.  There is no evidence of invasion into the submucosa.  Evidence of invasion may refer to stromal invasion by single cells or clusters of cells, presence of atypical and complex glandular architectural patterns beyond those that are present in normal mucosa, desmoplasia, and/or vascular invasion.

name: Intramucosal Lesion
definitions: A pathologic process that is confined to the mucosa.

name: Invasive Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that is not confined to the epithelium, and has spread to the surrounding stroma.

name: Invasive Lesion
definitions: A malignant cellular population that has increased in size and infiltrates the surrounding tissues.

name: Irinotecan/Panitumumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan and panitumumab that can be used for the treatment of colorectal cancer and appendiceal cancer.

name: Irinotecan/Panitumumab/Vemurafenib Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan, panitumumab and vemurafenib that can be used for the treatment of colon cancer.

name: Irinotecan/Ramucirumab Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan and ramucirumab that is used for the treatment of appendiceal adenocarcinoma, colon, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric and rectal cancers.

name: Irinotecan/Ziv-aflibercept Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of irinotecan and ziv-aflibercept that is used for the treatment of appendiceal adenocarcinoma and colorectal cancer.

name: Jaundice
definitions: A condition in which the skin and the whites of the eyes become yellow, urine darkens, and the color of stool becomes lighter than normal. Jaundice occurs when the liver is not working properly or when a bile duct is blocked. | Yellow pigmentation of the skin, mucous membranes, and the eyes due to hyperbilirubinemia. | Yellow pigmentation of the skin, mucous membranes, and the eyes due to hyperbilirubinemia. Causes include liver disease, biliary tract obstruction, and hemolysis. | Yellowish pigmentation of the skin, mucous membranes, and the eyes.

name: Juvenile Polyposis Syndrome
definitions: An autosomal dominant hereditary neoplastic syndrome. Mutations in the SMAD4 or BMPR1A genes have been identified in some patients. It is characterized by the presence of multiple juvenile polyps in the gastrointestinal tract.

name: KRAS Gene
definitions: A gene that may cause cancer when it is mutated (changed). The Kras gene makes the KRAS protein, which is involved in cell signaling pathways, cell growth, and apoptosis (cell death). Agents that block the activity of the mutated Kras gene or its protein may stop the growth of cancer. | This gene is involved in signal transduction and apoptosis.

name: KRAS Gene Amplification
definitions: A molecular genetic abnormality indicating the presence of multiple copies of the KRAS gene.

name: KRAS wt Allele
definitions: Human KRAS wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 12p12.1 and is approximately 45 kb in length. This allele, which encodes GTPase KRas protein, plays a role in the modulation of both cellular proliferation and differentiation. Mutations in the gene are associated with juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia, Noonan syndrome 3 and cardiofaciocutaneous syndrome.

name: Krukenberg Tumor
definitions: A tumor in the ovary caused by the spread of stomach cancer. | Metastatic signet-ring cell carcinoma in the ovary. The primary site is the gastrointestinal tract or breast.

name: Large Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that is composed of malignant large epithelial cells. This category includes large cell undifferentiated carcinomas, large cell differentiated carcinomas including large cell neuroendocrine carcinomas, and giant cell carcinomas.

name: Large Intestinal Mucosa
definitions: Mucosa consisting of the epithelium, basement membrane, lamina propria mucosae and lamina muscularis mucosae of the distal portion of the digestive tract from the small intestine to the anus.

name: Large Intestinal Wall Tissue
definitions: The tissue of the large intestine. It is composed of four layers - mucosa, submucosa, smooth muscle with inner circular and outer longitudinal layers, and serosa. The mucosa has a large number of goblet cells but does not have any villi. The longitudinal muscle layer is incomplete. The longitudinal muscle is limited to three distinct bands, called teniae coli that run the entire length of the colon. Contraction of the teniae coli exerts pressure on the wall and creates a series of pouches, called haustra, along the colon. Epiploic appendages, pieces of fat-filled connective tissue, are attached to the outer surface of the colon.

name: Large Intestine
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: A muscular tube that extends from the end of the small intestine to the anus. | A subdivision of the digestive tract that connects the small intestine to the cloaca or anus. Lacks or has few villi. | The avillous section of the intestine composed of crypts and extending from the terminal small intestine to the external orifice. | The long, tube-like organ that is connected to the small intestine at one end and the anus at the other. The large intestine has four parts: cecum, colon, rectum, and anal canal. Partly digested food moves through the cecum into the colon, where water and some nutrients and electrolytes are removed. The remaining material, solid waste called stool, moves through the colon, is stored in the rectum, and leaves the body through the anal canal and anus.

name: Limited Stage
definitions: A term used to describe cancer that is early in its growth, and may not have spread to other parts of the body. What is called early stage may differ between cancer types. | Cancer that is localized in its original site of growth, or involves adjacent lymph nodes but has not spread to other anatomic sites.

name: Liver
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: A large organ located in the upper abdomen. The liver cleanses the blood and aids in digestion by secreting bile. | A triangular-shaped organ located under the diaphragm in the right hypochondrium. It is the largest internal organ of the body, weighting up to 2 kg. Metabolism and bile secretion are its main functions. It is composed of cells which have the ability to regenerate. | An abdominal organ that has variable lobation which are composed mainly of hepatic lobules. | An exocrine gland which secretes bile and functions in metabolism of protein and carbohydrate and fat, synthesizes substances involved in the clotting of the blood, synthesizes vitamin A, detoxifies poisonous substances, stores glycogen, and breaks down worn-out erythrocytes.

name: Localized Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A digestive system carcinoma that is confined to a specific site without evidence of spread to other anatomic sites.

name: Localized Disease
definitions: A disease that is confined to a specific organ or tissue and has not spread to other anatomic sites.

name: Locally Advanced Adenocarcinoma
definitions: Adenocarcinoma that has spread to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.

name: Locally Advanced Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: Digestive system carcinoma that has spread to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.

name: Locally Advanced Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A squamous cell carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes.

name: Locally Advanced Unresectable Digestive System Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A digestive system adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes and is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Locally Advanced Unresectable Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A digestive system carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to nearby tissues or lymph nodes and is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Locally Recurrent Carcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of carcinoma after a period of remission, at or adjacent to the site of the original tumor.

name: Locally Recurrent Disease
definitions: The return of a disease after a period of remission at the site in which it initially manifested or adjacent to it.

name: Low Grade Lesion
definitions: A cancerous lesion with histological features of low grade.

name: Low Grade Neuroendocrine Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic lesion with neuroendocrine differentiation and histological features of low grade.

name: Low Mitotic Activity
definitions: Decreased frequency of cellular mitosis.

name: Lower Gastrointestinal Hemorrhage
definitions: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the lower gastrointestinal tract (small intestine, large intestine, and anus). | Bleeding from the lower gastrointestinal tract (small intestine, large intestine, and anus).

name: Lung
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: A thoracic organ that has variable lobation and is the primary respiratory organ of mammals. | One of a pair of organs in the chest that supplies the body with oxygen, and removes carbon dioxide from the body. | One of a pair of viscera occupying the pulmonary cavities of the thorax, the organs of respiration in which aeration of the blood takes place. As a rule, the right lung is slightly larger than the left and is divided into three lobes (an upper, a middle, and a lower or basal), while the left has two lobes (an upper and a lower or basal). Each lung is irregularly conical in shape, presenting a blunt upper extremity (the apex), a concave base following the curve of the diaphragm, an outer convex surface (costal surface), an inner or mediastinal surface (mediastinal surface), a thin and sharp anterior border, and a thick and rounded posterior border. | Respiration organ that develops as an outpocketing of the esophagus.

name: Lynch 1 Syndrome
definitions: A rare genetic neoplastic syndrome with an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance but incomplete penetrance. It is associated with a greater than 70 % risk of developing colorectal carcinoma. It is caused by a mutation in one of the mismatch repair genes: MSH2, MLH1, MSH6 or PMS2. It usually manifests at age 50 or younger with multiple synchronous or metachronous colorectal carcinomas. Clinical course is rapidly progressive. Prognosis is variable with a high risk for the development of additional colorectal carcinomas. However, survival is significantly better than non-HNPCC carcinomas of equivalent stage.

name: Lynch 2 Syndrome
definitions: A rare genetic neoplastic syndrome with an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance but incomplete penetrance. It is associated with an inherited risk for malignancy, in particular, colorectal, endometrial or gastric carcinoma. It is caused by mutations in one of the mismatch repair genes: MSH2, MLH1, MSH6 or PMS2. It usually manifests at age 50 or younger with multiple synchronous or metachronous proximal colonic or extracolonic cancers. Clinical course is rapidly progressive. Prognosis is variable with high risk for development of gastrointestinal, reproductive or urinary tract cancer. However, survival is significantly better than non-HNPCC carcinomas of equivalent stage.

name: Lynch Syndrome
definitions: An autosomal dominant hereditary neoplastic syndrome characterized by the development of colorectal carcinoma and a high risk of developing endometrial carcinoma, gastric carcinoma, ovarian carcinoma, renal pelvis carcinoma, and small intestinal carcinoma. Patients often develop colorectal carcinomas at an early age (mean, 45 years). In the majority of the cases the lesions arise from the proximal colon. At the molecular level, high-frequency microsatellite instability is present.

name: MBD4-Associated Neoplasia Syndrome
definitions: A syndrome inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and caused by germline mutations in MBD4 gene. It is characterized by the presence of multiple colorectal adenomatous polyps and an increased risk of developing colorectal cancer, acute myeloid leukemia, and uveal melanoma.

name: MCC Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the negative regulation of cell cycle progression.

name: MCC wt Allele
definitions: Human MCC wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 5q21-q22 and is approximately 360 kb in length. This allele, which encodes colorectal mutant cancer protein, may play a role in tumor suppression. MCC wild-type allele is a candidate colorectal tumor suppressor gene.

name: MIR124-1 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a tumor suppressor role in lung, colorectal and breast carcinomas, leukemia, medulloblastoma and glioblastoma multiforme.

name: MIR124-1 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIR124-1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 8p23.1 and is 84 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR124-1 RNA, plays a role in many cancers, including lung, colorectal, breast, leukemia, meduloblastoma and glioblastoma multiforme.

name: MIR126 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a role in the development of hepatocellular, lung, colon, breast and prostate carcinomas.

name: MIR126 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIR126 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 9q34.3 and is approximately 84 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR126 pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with the development of cancers of the lung, liver, colon, breast and prostate as well as leukemias.

name: MIR127 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a role in the development of hepatocellular, colon, bladder, breast and prostate carcinomas.

name: MIR127 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIR127 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 14q32.31 and is approximately 96 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR127 pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with the development of cancers of the liver, colon, bladder, breast and prostate.

name: MIR143 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a role in the development of colorectal and bladder carcinomas as well as Burkitt lymphoma, Diffuse large B-Cell lymphoma and chronic lymphocytic leukemia.

name: MIR143 wt Allele
definitions: Human MIR143 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 5q33.1 and is 105 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR143 pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with the development of bladder and colorectal cancer, Burkitt lymphoma, diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, chronic lymphocytic leukemia and obesity.

name: MIR145 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a role in the development of colorectal and prostate carcinomas as well as chronic lymphocytic leukemia, Burkitt lymphoma and diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

name: MIR145 wt Allele
definitions: Human MIR145 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 5q33.1 and is 87 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR145 pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with the development of prostate and colorectal cancers as well as chronic lymphocytic leukemia, Burkitt lymphoma, and diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.

name: MIR17 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays an oncogenic role in hepatocellular, lung, colorectal, thyoid and breast carcinomas, various leukemias and lymphomas, and neuroblastoma..

name: MIR17 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIR17 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 13q31.3 and is 83 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR17 pre-miRNA, plays a role in many cancers, including lung, liver, colorectal, thyroid, breast, neuroblastoma, leukemia and lymphoma.

name: MIR342 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a role in the development of colorectal carcinoma.

name: MIR342 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIR342 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 14q32.2 and is approximately 98 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR342 pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with development of colorectal cancer.

name: MIR34B Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression a plays a potential tumor suppressor role in colorectal carcinoma.

name: MIR34B wt Allele
definitions: Human MIR34B wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 11q23.1 and is 83 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR34B pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with development of colorectal, ovarian and non-small cell lung cancers, as well as Burkitt lymphoma.

name: MIR34C Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression a plays a potential tumor suppressor role in colorectal carcinoma.

name: MIR34C wt Allele
definitions: Human MIR34C wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 11q23.1 and is 83 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIR34C pre-miRNA, plays a role in the regulation of gene expression. Alteration in the expression of this gene is associated with colorectal, ovarian and non-small cell lung cancers.

name: MIRLET7A1 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a tumor suppressor role in a broad variety of carcinomas, hematopoietic cancers and other cancers.

name: MIRLET7A1 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIRLET7A1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 9q22.32 and is 79 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIRLET7A1 RNA, plays a role in development of many cancers including lung, liver, stomach, ovary, breast, colorectal, melanoma, leukemia, lymphoma, cholangiocarcinoma, glioblastoma, lipoma, myoma, hamartoma, sarcoma, adenoma and squamous carcinoma.

name: MIRLET7A3 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in the regulation of gene expression and plays a tumor suppressor role in a broad variety of carcinomas, hematopoietic cancers and other cancers.

name: MIRLET7A3 wt Allele
definitions: The human MIRLET7A3 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 22q13.31 and is 73 bases in length. This allele, which encodes MIRLET7A3 RNA, plays a role in development of many cancers including lung, liver, stomach ovary, breast, colorectal, melanoma, leukemia, lymphoma, cholangiocarcinoma, glioblastoma, lipoma, myoma, and hamartoma, sarcoma, adenoma and squamous carcinoma.

name: MUTYH-Associated Polyposis
definitions: An autosomal recessive hereditary neoplastic syndrome caused by mutations in the MUTYH gene on chromosome 1p34.1. It is characterized by the presence of multiple colorectal polyps that may progress to carcinoma. Development of gastric and small intestinal polyps may also occur.

name: MYC Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of the MYC gene.

name: Malignant Cecum Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the cecum.  Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

name: Malignant Cell
definitions: Cells of, or derived from, a malignant tumor.

name: Malignant Cellular Infiltrate
definitions: The spread of cancerous cells from the original site of growth to surrounding tissues.

name: Malignant Colon Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the colon.  Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

name: Malignant Colorectal Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the colon or rectum.  Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

name: Malignant Epithelial Cell with Vesicular Nucleus, Distinct Nucleolus, and Abundant Pink Cytoplasm
Malignant Rectal Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the rectum. Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

name: Malignant Rectosigmoid Neoplasm
definitions: A primary or metastatic malignant neoplasm that affects the rectosigmoid junction. Representative examples include carcinoma, lymphoma, and sarcoma.

name: Mapped ICDO3.1 Morphology PT Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to the Morphology preferred terms (PTs) with equivalent meaning in ICD-O-3.1.

name: Mapped ICDO3.1 Morphology Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to the Morphology terms with equivalent meaning in ICD-O-3.1.

name: Mapped ICDO3.1 Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to terms with equivalent meaning in the International Classification of Disease for Oncology third edition first revision (ICD-O-3.1).

name: Mapped ICDO3.2 Morphology PT Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to the Morphology preferred terms (PTs) with equivalent meaning in ICD-O-3.2.

name: Mapped ICDO3.2 Morphology Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to the Morphology terms with equivalent meaning in ICD-O-3.2.

name: Mapped ICDO3.2 Terminology
definitions: The set of terms that have been mapped to terms with equivalent meaning in the International Classification of Disease for Oncology third edition second revision (ICD-O-3.2).

name: Metastatic Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site.

name: Metastatic Carcinoma in the Liver
definitions: A carcinoma that has spread to the liver from another primary anatomic site.  Representative examples include metastatic carcinomas from the colon, breast, and lung.

name: Metastatic Carcinoma in the Lung
definitions: The spread of a carcinoma to the lung. This may be from a primary lung carcinoma, or from a carcinoma at a distant anatomic site.

name: Metastatic Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from the gastrointestinal system and has metastasized to other anatomic sites.

name: Metastatic Disease
definitions: A disease that has become metastatic, that is arising at a location apart from the primary lesion. | A finding indicating that a tumor has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site.

name: Metastatic Malignant Colorectal Neoplasm
definitions: A malignant neoplasm that arises in the colon or rectum that has spread from its original site of growth to other anatomic sites.

name: Metastatic Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that arises from squamous cells and has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site.

name: Metastatic Unresectable Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that has spread from its original site of growth to another anatomic site and is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Micropapillary Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of neoplastic glandular epithelial cells arranged in micropapillary patterns lacking fibrovascular cores.

name: Micropapillary Growth Pattern
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a growth pattern dominated by the presence of small papillary structures.

name: Microsatellite Stable
definitions: Indicates that no positive loci have been detected in the microsatellite instability (MSI) biomarkers in a sample.

name: Mismatch Repair Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of a gene involved in DNA mismatch repair.

name: Mismatch Repair Proficient
definitions: A finding indicating that a sample has normal concentrations of unmutated mismatch repair proteins and is either microsatellite stable or has low microsatellite instability.

name: Mitotic Checkpoint Serine/Threonine-Protein Kinase BUB1
definitions: Mitotic checkpoint serine/threonine-protein kinase BUB1 (1085 aa, ~122 kDa) is encoded by the human BUB1 gene. This protein is involved in both protein phosphorylation and mitotic prometaphase.

name: Mixed Cellular Population
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a cellular infiltrate with a mixed population of cells in a tissue sample.

name: Mixed Malignant Epithelial Cell Population
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a mixed population of malignant epithelial cells in a tumor sample.

name: Mixed Neoplastic Glandular and Squamous Cell Population
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating both glandular and squamous epithelial cells in a tumor specimen.

name: Moderately Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of histopathological features that are intermediate between well differentiated and poorly differentiated.

name: Moderately Differentiated Lesion
definitions: A lesion composed of cells that exhibit moderately differentiated features. | Of, or pertaining to, a lesion or cells that have some but not all characteristics of the normal origin tissue.

name: Modified FOLFOX Regimen
definitions: A modified version of the FOLFOX regimen consisting of leucovorin, infusional fluorouracil and oxaliplatin that may be used in the treatment of colorectal cancer.

name: Modified FOLFOX6 Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of leucovorin, infusional fluorouracil and oxaliplatin used for the treatment of anal carcinoma, esophageal and esophagogastric junction, gastric, occult primary, colorectal cancers, and appendiceal, small bowel, non-urothelial and urothelial bladder cancer, well-differentiated grade 3 neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), NETs of the pancreas, and extrapulmonary poorly differentiated neuroendocrine carcinoma/large or small cell carcinoma/mixed neuroendocrine-non-neuroendocrine neoplasm. This modified version differs from the FOLFOX6 regimen in that the dose of oxaliplatin is lower.
