name: Mothers Against Decapentaplegic Homolog 3
definitions: Mothers against decapentaplegic homolog 3 (425 aa, ~48 kDa) is encoded by the human SMAD3 gene. This protein plays a role in the modulation of transforming growth factor (TGF)-beta-mediated signaling and gene expression.

name: Mucinous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A type of cancer that begins in cells that line certain internal organs and produce mucin (the main component of mucus). | An adenocarcinoma comprising neoplastic glandular cells containing intracytoplasmic mucin. | An invasive adenocarcinoma composed of malignant glandular cells that contain intracytoplasmic mucin. Often, the infiltrating glandular structures are associated with mucoid stromal formation. It may arise from the large and small intestine, appendix, stomach, lung, ovary, breast, corpus uteri, cervix, vagina, and salivary gland.

name: Mucinous Differentiation
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating that non-mucinous tumor cells are developing the characteristic features of mucinous cells.

name: Mucoid Stroma Formation
definitions: A morphological finding indicating the presence of newly formed hyalinized, mucoid stromal cells. This is a characteristic finding in clear cell carcinomas.

name: NCI CTEP SDC Colorectal Cancer Sub-Category Terminology
definitions: Colorectal Cancer is an NCI Cancer Therapeutic Evaluation Program (CTEP) Simplified Disease Classification (SDC) category used to organize cancer-related disease coding that harmonizes with and supports reporting based on the global standard Medical Dictionary for Drug Regulatory Reporting (MedDRA) terminology.

name: NCI CTEP Simplified Disease Classification Terminology
definitions: The NCI Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP) Simplified Disease Classification (SDC), a three level hierarchy of Category, Sub-Category, and coding Term intended to provide an intuitive classification of neoplastic diseases to meet the needs of CTEP while still allowing reporting based on the Medical Dictionary for Drug Regulatory Reporting (MedDRA) terminology.  All CTEP SDC Terms are matched to NCIt concepts, which are linked to SDC Categories and Sub-Categories through Concept_In_Subset association relationships.

name: NCIt Neoplasm Core Terminology
definitions: A core reference set of NCIt neoplasm classification concepts to facilitate consistent coding, analysis, and data sharing across a broad range of NCI and related resources.

name: NICHD Terminology
definitions: Core library of terms associated with pediatrics, representing information related to child health and development from pre-birth through 21 years of age; contributed by the National Institute of Child Health and Human Development.

name: NTHL1-Related Tumor Syndrome
definitions: A syndrome inherited in an autosomal recessive manner and caused by germline bi-allelic mutations in NTHL1 gene. It is characterized by the presence of multiple colorectal adenomatous polyps and an increased risk of developing colorectal cancer, breast cancer, endometrial cancer, and less often cancers in other anatomic sites.

name: Neck
definitions: An organism subdivision that extends from the head to the pectoral girdle, encompassing the cervical vertebral column. | The region that connects the head to the rest of the body. | The region that connects the head to the rest of the body. (NCI)

name: Necrosis
definitions: A finding indicating the presence of cellular necrosis in a tissue specimen.

name: Neoplastic Cell
definitions: Cells of, or derived from, a tumor.

name: Neoplastic Epithelial Cell with Intracytoplasmic Mucin
Neoplastic Smooth Muscle Cell
definitions: A neoplastic mesenchymal cell that originates from a smooth muscle cell.

name: Neuroendocrine Differentiation
definitions: A finding in a tissue sample indicating the presence of epithelial cells that express neuroendocrine markers by immunohistochemistry (e.g., chromogranin A and neuron-specific enolase) and/or neurosecretory granules by electron microscopy.

name: Neuroendocrine and Non-Neuroendocrine Differentiation Present in the Same Cells
definitions: A finding in a tissue sample indicating the co-expression of neuroendocrine and non-neuroendocrine biomarkers in the same epithelial cells.

name: Neuroendocrine and Non-Neuroendocrine Neoplastic Components Present
definitions: A microscopic finding indicating the presence of two cellular neoplastic components, neuroendocrine and non-neuroendocrine, in a tissue sample.

name: Neuron-Specific Enolase Positive Neoplastic Cells Present
definitions: An immunophenotypic test result indicating positive staining of neoplastic cells for neuron-specific enolase.

name: No Evidence of Metastatic Disease
definitions: Diagnostic tests fail to detect presence of metastatic lesions.

name: Non-Invasive Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic process that is confined to the site of origin without morphologic evidence of cellular infiltration of the surrounding tissues.

name: Oligometastatic Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that has metastasized to a limited number of sites.

name: Oligometastatic Disease
definitions: A finding of a malignant solid neoplasm that has metastasized to a limited number of sites-existing in a state intermediate between localized disease and widespread metastatic disease.

name: Oncogene N-RAS
definitions: Human Oncogene N-RAS is a mutated variant of NRAS Gene (RAS Family), which encodes p21 N-Ras Protein, a monomeric GTPase involved in transmembrane signal transduction that alternates between inactive GDP-bound and active GTP-bound forms. RAS is activated by a guanine nucleotide-exchange factor and inactivated by a GTPase-activating protein. Mitogen-stimulated RAS stabilizes MYC protein and enhances MYC accumulation by the RAS/RAF/MAPK pathway, which appears to inhibit the proteasome-dependent degradation of MYC. Implicated in a variety of human tumors, specific amino acid mutations activate c-RAS and transform cells. Oncogene NRAS disrupts normal cell function.

name: PDGFRL Gene
definitions: This gene may play a role in the negative regulation of cell growth.

name: PDGFRL wt Allele
definitions: Human PDGFRL wild-type allele is located within 8p22-p21.3 and is approximately 66 kb in length. This allele, which encodes platelet-derived growth factor receptor-like protein, plays a putative role in the inhibition of cell growth and proliferation. Loss of PDGFRL gene function is associated with several types of cancer including colorectal cancers, hepatocellular carcinomas and non-small cell lung carcinomas.

name: PDZK1IP1 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in plasma membrane interactions.

name: PDZK1IP1 wt Allele
definitions: Human PDZK1IP1 wild-type allele is located within 1p33 and is approximately 7 kb in length. This allele, which encodes PDZK1-interacting protein 1, plays a role in plasma membrane interactions.

name: POLD1 Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of the POLD1 gene.

name: POLE Gene Mutation
definitions: A change in the nucleotide sequence of the POLE gene.

name: PTPN12 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the regulation of cell shape and mobility.

name: PTPN12 wt Allele
definitions: Human PTPN12 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 7q11.23 and is approximately 103 kb in length. This allele, which encodes tyrosine-protein phosphatase non-receptor type 12 protein, plays a role in the dephosphorylation of protein-tyrosine phosphates. Defects in the PTPN12 gene are associated with colon cancer.

name: PTPRJ Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the negative regulation of T cell receptor signaling.

name: PTPRJ wt Allele
definitions: Human PTPRJ wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 11p11.2 and is approximately 188 kb in length. This allele, which encodes receptor-type tyrosine-protein phosphatase eta protein, is involved in the dephosphorylation of protein-tyrosine phosphates. Defects in the PTPRJ gene are associated with several types of cancer such as breast, colon and lung cancers.

name: Papillary Growth Pattern
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a cellular infiltrate with papillary growth in a tissue sample.

name: Pediatric Hematology-Oncology Terminology
definitions: Terminology developed by a collaborative effort of the National Cancer Institute's Enterprise Vocabulary Services, the National Institute of Child Health and Human Development, and an international group of clinicians and informaticists to support the reporting of events observed in clinical care and research in children and adolescents seen by experts in blood disorders and cancer.

name: Platelet-Derived Growth Factor Receptor-Like Protein
definitions: Platelet-derived growth factor receptor-like protein (375 aa, ~42 kDa) is encoded by the human PDGFRL gene. This protein may be involved in signaling and tumor suppression.

name: Polygonal Adenocarcinoma Cell with Eosinophilic Cytoplasm
Polymerase Proofreading-Associated Polyposis
definitions: An autosomal dominant inherited neoplastic syndrome caused by germline mutations in the DNA polymerase proofreading genes POLE or POLD1. It is characterized by the presence of multiple colorectal adenomatous polyps and is associated with an increased risk of developing colorectal cancer, endometrial cancer, and less often gastric cancer, duodenal cancer, pancreatic cancer, breast cancer, and ovarian cancer.

name: Polyp
definitions: A growth that protrudes from a mucous membrane. | A usually exophytic mass attached to the underlying tissue by a broad base or a thin stalk.  Polyps can be neoplastic or non-neoplastic.  Neoplastic polyps usually represent proliferations of the epithelium, and are commonly seen in the gastrointestinal tract.  Polyps of the gastrointestinal tract are often called adenomas, are associated with dysplasia, and may eventually transform into carcinomas.  Non-neoplastic polyps may be inflammatory, degenerative, or the result of malformations.

name: Poorly Cohesive Malignant Cellular Infiltrate
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a malignant cellular infiltrate characterized by the presence of individual cells and/or tiny cellular aggregates.

name: Poorly Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of poorly differentiated histopathological features.

name: Poorly Differentiated Lesion
definitions: A lesion comprised of neoplastic cells that generally have lost most of the morphological features that are indicative of normal cells. | Of, or pertaining to, a lesion or cells that have little to no characteristics of the normal origin tissue.

name: Poorly Differentiated Neuroendocrine Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic lesion with neuroendocrine differentiation and poorly differentiated histological features.

name: Premalignant Lesion
definitions: A lesion characterized by the proliferation of atypical or dysplastic cells without evidence of stromal invasion.

name: Primary Lesion
definitions: A term that refers to a pathologic process in its original anatomic site of growth. | The identification of a tumor, lesion, or site of disease that is present at the site of origin.

name: Proximal Colon
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: The part of colon that includes the cecum, ascending colon, and hepatic flexure.

name: Questionable Invasive Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic lesion in which the adjacent tissues, surrounding capsule, or vessels may or may not be invaded by neoplastic cells.

name: RAD54B Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in DNA repair and mitotic/meiotic recombination.

name: RAD54B wt Allele
definitions: Human RAD54B wild-type allele is located within 8q21.3-q22 and is approximately 103 kb in length. This allele, which encodes DNA repair and recombination protein RAD54B, plays a role in both mitotic recombination and DNA repair. Certain allelic variants of the RAD54B gene are associated with several types of cancer such as colon cancer and primary lymphoma.

name: RAD54L Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in the homologous recombination of double-strand DNA breaks.

name: RAD54L wt Allele
definitions: Human RAD54L wild-type allele is located within 1p32 and is approximately 31 kb in length. This allele, which encodes DNA repair and recombination protein RAD54-like protein, is involved in the homologous recombination related repair of DNA double-strand breaks.

name: Rare Lesion
definitions: A lesion that is uncommonly seen.

name: Rectal Adenomatous Polyp
definitions: A polypoid adenoma that arises from, and protrudes into the lumen of, the rectum.

name: Rectal Hemorrhage
definitions: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the rectal wall and discharged from the anus. | Bleeding originating from the rectal wall and discharged from the anus.

name: Rectal Neuroendocrine Neoplasm
definitions: A neoplasm with neuroendocrine differentiation that arises from the rectum. It includes neuroendocrine tumors (well-differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms) and neuroendocrine carcinomas (poorly differentiated neuroendocrine neoplasms).

name: Rectosigmoid Region
definitions: An anatomical junction that is between the sigmoid colon and rectum. | The area where the sigmoid colon joins the rectum.

name: Rectum
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: The last several inches of the large intestine closest to the anus. | The terminal portion of the gastrointestinal tract, extending from the rectosigmoid junction to the anal canal. | The terminal portion of the intestinal tube, terminating with the anus. | The terminal portion of the large intestine extending from the terminus of the colon to the anus or anal canal.

name: Recurrent Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that has recurred after a period of remission.

name: Recurrent Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: The reemergence of carcinoma in any part of the digestive system after a period of remission.

name: Recurrent Disease
definitions: The return of a disease after a period of remission. | The return of a disease or the signs and symptoms of a disease after a period of improvement.

name: Recurrent Paget Disease of the Anal Canal
definitions: The reemergence of Paget disease of the anal canal after a period of remission.

name: Recurrent Unresectable Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma that has reemerged after a period of remission and is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Refractory Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that does not respond to treatment.

name: Refractory Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma hat arises from any part of the digestive system and is resistant to treatment.

name: Refractory Disease
definitions: A disease that is not responsive to treatment. | A disease that resists treatment.

name: Refractory Metastatic Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A digestive system carcinoma that has metastasized to another anatomic site and is resistant to treatment.

name: Refractory Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: Squamous cell carcinoma that is resistant to treatment.

name: Resectable Digestive System Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A digestive system adenocarcinoma that is amenable to surgical resection.

name: Resectable Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A digestive system carcinoma that is amenable to surgical resection.

name: Resectable Mass
definitions: A mass that is amenable to surgical resection.

name: Respiratory System
definitions: Functional system which consists of structures involved in respiration. | The organs and anatomic structures involved in inspiration and expiration of air and the exchange of carbon dioxide and oxygen. | The organs involved in the exchange of carbon dioxide and oxygen including the trachea, bronchi, and lung parenchyma. | The organs that are involved in breathing. These include the nose, throat, larynx, trachea, bronchi, and lungs.

name: Rho GTPase-Activating Protein 7
definitions: Rho GTPase-activating protein 7 (1528 aa, ~171 kDa) is encoded by the human DLC1 gene. This protein is involved in the mediation of both signal transduction and cytoskeletal rearrangement.

name: SMAD2 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in signal transduction and repression of transcription.

name: SMAD2 wt Allele
definitions: Human SMAD2 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 18q21.1 and is approximately 93 kb in length. This allele, which encodes mothers against decapentaplegic homolog 2 protein, plays a role in the potentiation of transforming growth factor-beta signaling; and thus, regulates cellular proliferation, apoptosis, and differentiation.

name: Sarcomatoid Carcinoma
definitions: A malignant epithelial neoplasm characterized by the presence of spindle cells and anaplastic morphologic features.  Giant cells and a sarcomatous component may also be present. | A malignant epithelial neoplasm characterized by the presence of spindle cells. | A type of cancer that begins in the skin or in tissues that line or cover internal organs and that contains long spindle-shaped cells.

name: Sarcomatous Component Present
definitions: A histopathologic finding characterized by the presence of malignant cells of connective tissue origin in a tumor sample.  Examples of connective tissue cells that can undergo malignant transformation include smooth and skeletal muscle cells, adipose tissue cells, osteocytes, and chondrocytes.

name: Secondary Lesion
definitions: A lesion that develops in an organ or tissue as a complication of another existing condition.

name: Serrated Glandular Architecture
definitions: The common saw-tooth architectural pattern observed within hyperplastic polyps.

name: Severe Cytologic Atypia
definitions: A finding indicating the presence of abnormal cells with severe architectural changes.

name: Severe Dysplasia
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of severe cellular atypia associated with severe architectural changes in a tissue sample.

name: Sigmoid Colon
semantic_types: Body Part, Organ, or Organ Component
definitions: The S-shaped section of the colon that connects to the rectum. | The part of the large intestine that is closest to the rectum and anus. It forms a loop that averages about 40 cm. in length, and normally lies within the pelvis, but on account of its freedom of movement it is liable to be displaced into the abdominal cavity. | The portion of the colon that connects to the descending colon above and the rectum below. | The portion of the colon that connects to the descending colon above and the rectum below. (NCI)

name: Signet Ring Adenocarcinoma Cell
definitions: A malignant glandular cell containing cytoplasmic mucin. The presence of mucin pushes the nucleus of the cell to the periphery, assuming a configuration resembling a signet ring.

name: Signet Ring Cell Adenocarcinoma
definitions: A highly malignant type of cancer typically found in glandular cells that line the digestive organs. The cells resemble signet rings when examined under a microscope. | A usually aggressive, poorly differentiated invasive adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of malignant glandular cells in which the nucleus is pressed to one side by the presence of intracytoplasmic mucus. It may arise from the stomach, small and large intestine, ampulla of Vater, appendix, gallbladder, pancreas, lung, bladder, breast, and prostate gland.

name: Solid Neoplasm
definitions: A benign or malignant neoplasm arising from tissues that do not include fluid areas.  Representative examples include epithelial neoplasms (e.g. lung carcinoma, prostate carcinoma, breast carcinoma, colon carcinoma), and neoplasms arising from the soft tissues and bones (e.g. leiomyosarcoma, liposarcoma, chondrosarcoma, osteosarcoma).  Neoplasms originating from the blood or bone marrow (leukemias and myeloproliferative disorders) are not considered solid tumors. | Abnormal mass of tissue that usually does not contain cysts or liquid areas. Solid tumors may be benign (not cancerous), or malignant (cancerous). Different types of solid tumors are named for the type of cells that form them. If tumors are associated with those under the term cancer, these terms should be used. | An abnormal mass of tissue that usually does not contain cysts or liquid areas. Solid tumors may be benign (not cancer), or malignant (cancer). Different types of solid tumors are named for the type of cells that form them. Examples of solid tumors are sarcomas, carcinomas, and lymphomas. Leukemias (cancers of the blood) generally do not form solid tumors.

name: Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A carcinoma arising from squamous epithelial cells.  Morphologically, it is characterized by the proliferation of atypical, often pleomorphic squamous cells.  Squamous cell carcinomas are graded by the degree of cellular differentiation as well, moderately, or poorly differentiated.  Well differentiated carcinomas are usually associated with keratin production and the presence of intercellular bridges between adjacent cells.  Representative examples are lung squamous cell carcinoma, skin squamous cell carcinoma, and cervical squamous cell carcinoma. | A malignant neoplasm arising from squamous epithelial cells. | Cancer that begins in squamous cells, which are thin, flat cells that look like fish scales. Squamous cells are found in the tissue that forms the surface of the skin, the lining of the hollow organs of the body, and the passages of the respiratory and digestive tracts.

name: Squamous Differentiation
definitions: A microscopic finding indicating the presence of cells with squamous differentiation in a tumor sample.

name: Stage 0
definitions: Cancer confined to the epithelium without invasion of the basement membrane.

name: Stage I
definitions: Invasive cancer confined to the original anatomic site of growth without lymph node involvement.

name: Stage II
definitions: Invasive cancer more extensive than stage I, usually involving local lymph nodes without spread to distant anatomic sites.

name: Stage IIA
definitions: Invasive cancer more extensive than stage I, without spread to distant anatomic sites. The definition of stage IIA depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for colorectal cancer stage IIA includes: T3, N0, M0. T3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)

name: Stage IIB
definitions: Invasive cancer more extensive than stage I without spread to distant anatomic sites. The definition of stage IIB depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for colorectal cancer, stage IIB includes: T4a, N0, M0. T4a: Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion of tumor through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum). N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)

name: Stage IIC
definitions: A cancer stage generally indicating the invasion of adjacent structures by cancer without distant metastases.

name: Stage III
definitions: Locally advanced cancer that has spread to nearby organs but not to distant anatomic sites.

name: Stage IIIA
definitions: Locally advanced cancer that has spread to nearby organs but not to distant anatomic sites. The definition of stage IIIA depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for bladder cancer, stage IIIA includes: (T3a, T3b, T4a, N0, M0); (T1-T4a, N1, M0). T1: Tumor invades lamina propria (subepithelial connective tissue). T2: Tumor invades muscularis propria. T3: Tumor invades perivesical soft tissue. T3a: Tumor invades perivesical soft tissue, microscopically. T3b: Tumor invades perivesical soft tissue, macroscopically (extravesical mass). T4a: Extravesical tumor invades directly into prostatic stroma, uterus, vagina. N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. N1: Single regional lymph node metastasis in the true pelvis (perivesical, obturator, internal and external iliac, or sacral lymph node). M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th ed.)

name: Stage IIIB
definitions: Locally advanced cancer that has spread to nearby organs but not to distant anatomic sites. The definition of stage IIIB depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for testicular cancer, stage IIIB incudes: (Any pT/TX, N1-3, M0, S2); (Any pT/TX, Any N, M1a, S2). TX: Testicular cancer in which the primary tumor cannot be assessed. N1: Testicular cancer with metastasis with a lymph node mass 2 cm or smaller in greatest dimension and less than or equal to five nodes positive, none larger than 2 cm in greatest dimension. N2: Testicular cancer with metastasis with a lymph node mass larger than 2 cm but not larger than 5 cm in greatest dimension; or more than five nodes positive, none larger than 5 cm; or evidence of extranodal extension of tumor. N3: Testicular cancer with metastasis with a lymph node mass larger than 5 cm in greatest dimension. M0: Testicular cancer without evidence of distant metastasis. M1a: Testicular cancer with non-retroperitoneal nodal or pulmonary metastases. S2: LDH 1.5-10 x N or hCG (mlU/mL) 5,000-50,000 or AFP (ng/mL) 1,000-10,000. N indicates the upper limit of normal for the LDH assay. (AJCC 8th ed.)

name: Stage IIIC
definitions: Cancer that has spread to nearby organs but not to distant anatomic sites. The definition of stage IIIC depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for ovarian cancer, stage IIIC includes: T3c, N0/N1, M0. T3c: Ovarian cancer with macroscopic peritoneal metastasis beyond the pelvis more than 2 cm in greatest dimension with or without metastasis to the retroperitoneal lymph nodes (includes extension of tumor to capsule of liver and spleen without parenchymal involvement of either organ). N0: No regional lymph node metastasis. N1: Positive retroperitoneal lymph nodes only (histologically confirmed). M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th Ed.)

name: Stage IV
definitions: Advanced cancer that has spread to adjacent and/or distant anatomic sites.

name: Stage IVA
definitions: Advanced cancer. The definition of stage IVA depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for uterine corpus carcinoma or carcinosarcoma, stage IVA includes: T4, Any N, M0. T4: Uterine corpus carcinoma or carcinosarcoma with tumor invading the bladder mucosa and/or bowel mucosa (bullous edema is not sufficient to classify a tumor as T4). M0: No distant metastasis. (AJCC 8th Ed.)

name: Stage IVB
definitions: Advanced cancer. The definition of stage IVB depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for bone cancer, stage IVB includes: (Any T, N1, Any M, Any G); (Any T, Any N, M1b, Any G). N1: Regional lymph node metastasis. M1b: Metastasis in the bone or other distant sites. (AJCC 8th ed.)

name: Stage IVC
definitions: Cancer that has spread to distant anatomic sites beyond its original site of growth. The definition of stage IVC depends on the particular type of cancer that it refers to; for example, for head and neck carcinomas (lip and oral cavity carcinoma, laryngeal carcinoma, nasal cavity and paranasal sinus carcinoma, pharynx carcinoma, thyroid gland carcinoma, and major salivary gland carcinoma) and head and neck mucosal melanoma, stage IVC is defined as follows: any T, any N, M1; for appendix carcinoma, stage IVC is defined as follows: any T, any N, M1b, any G. M1: Distant metastasis.  M1b: Nonperitoneal metastasis.  G: Grade (cellular differentiation).

name: Submucosal Lesion
definitions: A pathologic process that affects the loose connective tissue beneath the mucosa.

name: TAP1 Gene
definitions: This gene is involved in peptide transport and antigen processing.

name: TAP1 wt Allele
definitions: Human TAP1 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 6p21.3 and is approximately 9 kb in length. This allele, which encodes antigen peptide transporter 1 protein, is involved in the presentation of antigen fragments to major histocompatibiltiy complex class I proteins. Mutations in the gene are associated with with ankylosing spondylitis, insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus, type I bare lymphocyte syndrome and celiac disease.

name: TCGA BCR Tumor Code Identifier
definitions: A two digit numerical identifier assigned to a specific tumor sample from a by the The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) Biospecimen Core Resource (BCR).

name: TP53 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a critical role in cell cycle regulation and has tumor suppressor activity.

name: TP53 Gene Inactivation
definitions: A mutation in the TP53 gene that either inhibits the expression of or results in the translation of an inactive cellular tumor antigen p53 protein.

name: TP53 wt Allele
definitions: A tumor suppressor gene that normally inhibits the growth of tumors. This gene is altered in many types of cancer. | Human TP53 wild-type allele is located in the vicinity of 17p13.1 and is approximately 19 kb in length. This allele, which encodes cellular tumor antigen p53 protein, plays a role in cell cycle regulation during the G0/G1transition. Alterations of the TP53 gene occur as both somatic and germline mutations in human malignancies in select cancer-prone families with Li-Fraumeni syndrome.

name: TP53*1 Allele
definitions: Human TP53*1 allele is located in the vicinity of 17p13.1 and is approximately 19 kb in length. This allele, which encodes cellular tumor antigen p53*1 protein, is involved in apoptosis and tumor suppression. This variant exhibits a coding change from a proline at amino acid 72 to an arginine, which may affect the activity and stability of the encoded protein.

name: Tenesmus
definitions: Painful spasm of the anal sphincter along with an urgent desire to defecate without the significant production of feces; associated with irritable bowel syndrome.

name: Thoracic Cavity
definitions: The cavity enclosed by the ribs between the diaphragm and the neck. | The cavity in the vertebrate body enclosed by the ribs between the diaphragm and the neck and containing the lungs and heart.

name: Tissue
definitions: A group or layer of cells that work together to perform a specific function. | An anatomical structure consisting of similarly specialized cells and intercellular matrix, aggregated according to genetically determined spatial relationships, performing a specific function. | An anatomical structure consisting of similarly specialized cells and intercellular matrix, aggregated according to genetically determined spatial relationships, performing a specific function. (NCI)

name: Tubuloglandular Pattern
definitions: A morphologic finding indicating the presence of a tubuloglandular growth pattern in a cellular infiltrate.

name: Turcot Syndrome
definitions: An autosomal dominant hereditary neoplastic syndrome caused by mutations in the PMS2, MLH1, MSH2, or APC genes. There are two types described, type 1, characterized by the presence of glioblastoma and often associated with hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal carcinoma, and type 2, characterized by the presence of medulloblastoma and familiar adenomatous polyposis.

name: Turcot Syndrome Type 1
definitions: An autosomal dominant hereditary neoplastic syndrome caused by mutations in the PMS2, MLH1, or MSH2 genes. It is characterized by the presence of glioblastoma and the absence of familiar adenomatous polyposis. Patients often develop hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal carcinoma.

name: Turcot Syndrome Type 2
definitions: An autosomal dominant hereditary neoplastic syndrome caused by mutations in the APC gene. It is characterized by the presence of medulloblastoma and familiar adenomatous polyposis.

name: Tyrosine-Protein Phosphatase Non-Receptor Type 12
definitions: Tyrosine-protein phosphatase non-receptor type 12 (780 aa, ~88 kDa) is encoded by the human PTPN12 gene. This protein plays a role in both cytoskeleton morphology and protein dephosphorylation.

name: Ulcerative Colitis
definitions: A chronic inflammatory disease affecting the mucosal surface of the colon and rectum. | An inflammatory bowel disease involving the mucosal surface of the large intestine and rectum. It may present with an acute or slow onset and follows an intermittent or continuous course. Signs and symptoms include abdominal pain, diarrhea, fever, weight loss, and intestinal hemorrhage. | Chronic inflammation of the colon that produces ulcers in its lining.  This condition is marked by abdominal pain, cramps, and loose discharges of pus, blood, and mucus from the bowel.

name: Undifferentiated Lesion
definitions: A lesion composed of cells that exhibit undifferentiated features.

name: Unresectable Digestive System Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma that arises from the gastrointestinal system and is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Unresectable Digestive System Carcinoma
definitions: A digestive system carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Unresectable Mass
definitions: A mass that is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Unresectable Squamous Cell Carcinoma
definitions: A squamous cell carcinoma that is not amenable to surgical resection.

name: Villous Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of a villous architectural pattern. It may arise from a villous adenoma.

name: Villous Pattern
definitions: A morphological appearance resembling the villi seen on the mucosa of the small bowel

name: WNT3 Gene
definitions: This gene plays a role in signal transduction and intercellular communication. It is involved in neural development.

name: WNT3 wt Allele
definitions: Human WNT3 wild-type allele is located within 17q21-q22 and is approximately 54 kb in length. This allele, which encodes proto-oncogene wnt-3 protein, is involved in fetal development.

name: Weight Loss
definitions: A finding characterized by a decrease in overall body weight; for pediatrics, less than the baseline growth curve. | A reduction in total body weight.

name: Well Differentiated Adenocarcinoma
definitions: An invasive adenocarcinoma characterized by the presence of well differentiated histopathological features.

name: Well Differentiated Lesion
definitions: A lesion composed of cells that exhibit well differentiated features. | Of, or pertaining to, a lesion or cells that have most characteristics of the normal origin tissue.

name: Well Differentiated Neuroendocrine Lesion
definitions: A neoplastic lesion with neuroendocrine differentiation and well differentiated histological features.

name: XELOX Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of capecitabine and oxaliplatin used for the treatment of advanced stage colorectal cancer. This regimen differs from a similar regimen, CAPOX, with regards to the dosing schedule for oxaliplatin. | An abbreviation for a chemotherapy combination used to treat colorectal cancer that has spread. It is also being studied in the treatment of other types of cancer. It includes the drugs capecitabine (Xeloda) and oxaliplatin.

name: mCode Cancer Disorder Value Set
definitions: Terminology created by EVS to support the Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCode), a data standard for all cancer types.

name: mCode Carcinoma In-Situ Disorder Value Set
definitions: Terminology created by EVS to support the Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCode), a data standard for all cancer types.

name: mCode Elixhauser Malignant Solid Tumors in Situ Value Set
definitions: Terminology created by EVS to support the Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCode), a data standard for all cancer types.

name: mCode Primary Cancer Disorder Value Set
definitions: Terminology created by EVS to support the Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCode), a data standard for all cancer types.

name: mCode Terminology
definitions: Terminology created by EVS to support the Minimal Common Oncology Data Elements (mCode), a data standard for all cancer types.

name: mFOLFOX7 Regimen
definitions: A regimen consisting of a modified version of FOLFOX, which contains leucovorin, infusional fluorouracil and oxaliplatin, used for the treatment of appendiceal and small bowel adenocarcinomas, and colorectal cancer.
